calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-18-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-18-2019 Continued To Dec-04-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anthony Giles, Esq. 415-839-2099 for Plaintiff.
John Freed, Esq. of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP for Defendant PENGUIN RANDOM HOUSE through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT PENGUIN RANDOM HOUSE LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant Penguin Random House LLC's demurrer to Plaintiff Steven C. Wade's complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to all three causes of action for reformation of contract under Civil Code section 3399, breach of contract, and common count.

The first cause of action for contract reformation fails because Civil Code section 3399 is inapplicable. Section 3399 is used to reform a contract to reflect the shared intent of the parties at the time the contract was formed. (Civ. Code 3399; see Shupe v. Nelson (1967) 254 Cal.App.2d 693, 700 ["[T]he contract may be reformed to express a single intention entertained by both parties. Although a court of equity may revise a written instrument to make it conform to the real agreement, it has no power to make a new contract for the parties, whether the mistake be mutual or unilateral."].) Plaintiff concedes that the ultimate purchaser of Prima Games, Asteri Networks, was not contemplated by the parties or included in Schedule A of the Agreement when he and Defendant entered into the Agreement on February 14, 2018. (Compl. 5, Ex. A.) He also alleges that he did not attempt to add Asteri to the list until March 27, 2018. (Id. 6.) Plaintiff's failure to formally amend the Schedule is not a mistake or fraud that justifies reformation under Section 3399 because Plaintiff seeks to create a new agreement that was not initially agreed upon by the parties.

The second cause of action for breach of contract fails because the Agreement requires modifications to be confirmed in writing by Defendant, so any oral amendment was improper. (Compl. 6, 16, Ex. A 3b, 11.) Oral modifications of written agreements are precluded where the written agreement provides for written modification. (Civ. Code 1698(c); Conley v. Matthes (1997) 56 Cal.App.4th 1453, 1465.)

Finally, Plaintiff's third cause of action for common count seeks the same recovery as the defective breach of contract claim, so that claim must also fail. (See McBride v. Boughton (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 379, 394 ["When a common count is used as an alternative way of seeking the same recovery demanded in a specific cause of action, and is based on the same facts, the common count is demurrable if the cause of action is demurrable."].)

Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed in compliance with CRC 3.1312.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-22-2019 Continued To Sep-17-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

No appearances.

There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT PENGUIN RANDOM HOUSE LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to 9/17/19 per request of the parties.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
